programs.name: Brazos Valley Community Health Center
programs.eligibility: All patients welcome regardless of insurance or ability to pay. Sliding fee scale based on household income and size. Accepts Medicaid, Medicare, CHIP, and private insurance.

programs.name: Bluebonnet Community Health Center
programs.eligibility: Sliding fee discount for patients at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level. Accepts most insurance.

programs.name: Texas Health and Human Services (HHSC) - Brazos County Office
programs.eligibility: Income-based. Medicaid for low-income families, children, pregnant women, elderly, and disabled. CHIP for children in families earning too much for Medicaid.

programs.name: Brazos Valley Food Bank
programs.eligibility: Open to anyone in need. No income verification for emergency food.

programs.name: 340B Drug Pricing Program (via Brazos Valley CHC Pharmacy)
services: Discounted prescription drugs for qualifying patients
programs.eligibility: Must be an established patient of a 340B-covered entity (like BVCHC). Significant discounts on brand-name and generic medications.

programs.name: St. Joseph Health (CHI) - Financial Assistance Program
services: Free or discounted hospital care for qualifying patients
programs.eligibility: Patients at or below 400% of Federal Poverty Level may qualify for partial or full write-off. Application required.

programs.name: Baylor Scott & White - College Station Financial Assistance
programs.address: 700 Scott & White Dr, College Station, TX 77845
services: Financial assistance for hospital and clinic bills
programs.eligibility: Uninsured or underinsured patients meeting income guidelines up to 400% FPL.

programs.name: Texas A&M Health Community Health Worker Program
programs.eligibility: Residents of Brazos Valley, especially rural areas. Free service.

programs.name: NeedyMeds
programs.eligibility: Varies by program - many available to uninsured or underinsured. Free drug card available to everyone.

programs.name: GoodRx Prescription Savings
programs.address: Online - accepted at most US pharmacies
programs.eligibility: Open to everyone - no insurance needed. Free to use.

programs.name: Healthcare.gov Marketplace (ACA)
programs.eligibility: Most US citizens and legal residents. Subsidies available for households earning 100-400% FPL (expanded through 2025 - no upper cap on subsidy eligibility).

programs.name: Lone Star Legal Aid
services: Free legal help with medical debt
programs.eligibility: Low-income residents (generally at or below 125% FPL). Free services.
